entries.title: ERA Drive
entries.subtitle: Top Secret
entries.body:
The key property is Resonant Energy Transmission, when a crystal receives focused energy input it re-emits it to nearby crystals in a roughly 1km radius, with negligible loss. On it's own this is just useful for wireless power transmission. The FTL effect comes in from something different, when the energy density crossing through a crystal lattice exceeds a certain threshold the crystal doesn't transmit it, it breifly warps the local metric around itself. The current theory among Solveyran physicists is that the crystal lattice interacts with the energy in a way that creates a localized reduction in what they call 'relativistic drag', ie, the resistance that spacetime exerts on matter reaching light speed. Above the threshold this drag reduction becomes total and the object punches through rather then just approaching lightspeed.

However no single crystal can reach this threshold alone. It only emerges when multiple crystals are perfectly arranged in a focused array and their transmission fields overlap constructively. The original discovery was a massive ground based array focusing on a probe in low orbit, essentially an accidental particle accelerator built from the crystals.

However for a self contained drive the array has to travel with the vessel, which creates the core engineering challenge. The crystals need to focus energy inward on the vessel itself whilst the vessel is inside the array. Think of it less like a rocket engine and that the vessel is sitting inside a focusing lens made of crystals, with the energy fed from outside the array and concentrated at the center.

entries.title: Varn Dyson Swarm
entries.subtitle: Megastructure
entries.body:
The Varn Dyson Swarm is a complete stellar energy collection structure surrounding the red dwarf star 'Varn', consisting of thousands of satellites, all dedicated to collecting solar energy, surrounding the star. It is the largest engineering project undertaken by Solveyra and the foundation that keeps the nation's industry working.

The swarm exists for a singular purpose, to harvest energy from Varn and deliver it to Velundra. Each satellite in the network is a singular unit, equipped with both solar panels and a transmission device, which is used to beam the harvested energy back down to the collection towers on Velundra's surface. These towers are capped with a large Elenaric crystal array that receives the energy and routes it back into the grid below.

Construction of the swarm began following the discovery and large scale adoption of Elenaric Crystals as the basis of Solveyra's energy infrastructure. With a functional energy grid in place the logical next step was to harvest the power from the local star and thus the Swarm Project was approved by the council.

Construction happened over roughly 2 centuries using a self sustaining loop. Initial satellites were launched from the Energy collection towers using directed energy propulsion, these formed the first 'shell' around Varn. As that shell started to transmit energy that was used to power more launches and this happened over the course of these 2 centuries.

The swarm is composed of multiple concentric orbital shells at varying distances from Varn, each populated by thousands of smaller satellites. Individual units are rather simple in design, a solar panel array, an onboard power storage system, an energy transmission device and a propulsion system to maintain it's position. There is no central hub or coordinating superstructure as each satellite acts independently, transmitting on a scheduled cycle coordinated by ground based systems.

The receiving end of the network on Velundra's surface consists of the Energy collection towers, large industrial structures with 90% of their structure underwater, their cap being a large Elenaric Crystal Array which receives transmitted energy. These are the most visible part of Solveyran civilization on Velundra.

The swarm is maintained entirely by automated drone systems. No Velnorca has ever been in the swarm. Maintenance drones are launched from the Energy Collection Towers, they perform a regular inspection, doing any repairs needed, replacing damaged components and adjusting any orbital positions. The failure of any number of individual satellites has a negligible impact on the overall output given the scale and fluidity of the 'structure'.

The swarm does not have any economic cost in the conventional sense due to the lack of currency within Solveyran society. What it does require is labor through the contribution of engineers, drone operators, materials workers and scientists who maintain the ground system and automated maintenance fleet. This contribution is given freely in the same way all contribution in Solveyra is given.

The economic impact of the swarm has completely transformative, energy scarcity has always been a problem for Solveyra. The swarm eliminated said scarcity and allowed for untold expansion, both down and around, and the research impact was also great, paving the way for interstellar drones and the eventual accidental discovery of FTL.

The swarm's cultural impact is more practical then anything else. It's seen as vital infrastructure, not worshiped or romanticized. However it is still a sign of national pride and is routinely taught as an example of what Solveyra is capable of given commitment and patience.
